first_meeting.speaker: gerald
first_meeting.text: Oh! Oh, you must be Mr. Grimshaw! The detective! Please, please, you have to help me!

first_meeting_2.speaker: gerald
first_meeting_2.text: I've been to the police, the Labyrinth Board, the Magical Creatures Housing Association — nobody will do anything!

first_meeting_response.text: Slow down, big fella. Take a breath. What happened?

first_meeting_4.text: Your... labyrinth. Someone stole... an entire labyrinth.

first_meeting_5.speaker: gerald
first_meeting_5.text: Yes! I came home from the shops — I was buying thread, I go through a LOT of thread — and it was just... GONE.

first_meeting_6.speaker: gerald
first_meeting_6.text: The whole thing. Walls, corridors, dead ends, the lot. Just a big empty field with a 'FOR SALE' sign on it.

choices.text: Do you have any idea who took it?
What exactly IS a stolen labyrinth case?
I'll look into it. Sit tight.

who_took_it.speaker: gerald
who_took_it.text: I don't know! That's why I need a DETECTIVE!

who_took_it_2.speaker: gerald
who_took_it_2.text: Well... there was one thing. I found this business card on the ground where the entrance used to be.

who_took_it_3.speaker: gerald
who_took_it_3.text: 'Bastardo's Discount Labyrinth Relocation Services — We Move Your Maze!' With a little winky face.

who_took_it_4.text: Bastardo. Why is it always Bastardo.

who_took_it_6.text: Everyone knows The Magnificent Bastardo. Con artist, showman, and the most wanted wizard in three counties.

what_is_case.speaker: gerald
what_is_case.text: Well, it's— I mean— a labyrinth is a minotaur's HOME. It's our identity. Without it, I'm just... a large man with horns and a poor sense of direction.

what_is_case_2.speaker: gerald
what_is_case_2.text: Do you know how hard it is to get a flat when you're a minotaur? Landlords take one look at the horns and suddenly the property is 'no longer available.'

what_is_case_3.text: I hear you, Gerald. The housing market is rough for everyone. Especially those of us above kebab shops.

about_gerald.speaker: gerald
about_gerald.text: Oh, well, I'm Gerald. Just Gerald. I had a surname once but it was thirteen syllables long and nobody could pronounce it.

about_gerald_2.speaker: gerald
about_gerald_2.text: I'm a certified Labyrinth Dweller, Grade B. I did my NVQ in Lurking and everything. Got a distinction in Ominous Breathing.

about_gerald_3.speaker: gerald
about_gerald_3.text: I also do a bit of freelance menacing on weekends. Well, I DID. Hard to menace without a labyrinth. It's all about the acoustics.

sit_tight_bye.speaker: gerald
sit_tight_bye.text: Oh, thank you, Mr. Grimshaw! I'll just... wait here then, shall I?

sit_tight_2.text: That's literally what a waiting room is for, Gerald.

sit_tight_3.speaker: gerald
sit_tight_3.text: Right! Yes! Waiting. I can do that. I'm very good at waiting. Minotaurs wait a lot. In labyrinths. Which I DON'T HAVE.

return_greeting.speaker: gerald
return_greeting.text: Mr. Grimshaw! Any news? Have you found my labyrinth? Is it okay? Does it miss me?

choices.text: I'm working on it. Just need to sort out the paperwork.
Tell me about that business card again.
Sit tight, Gerald. I'll be back.

paperwork_response.speaker: gerald
paperwork_response.text: Paperwork? PAPERWORK?! My home has been STOLEN and there's PAPERWORK?!

paperwork_2.text: Welcome to the magical services industry, Gerald. Everything requires a form.

paperwork_3.speaker: gerald
paperwork_3.text: I hate paperwork. My hooves can't hold a pen properly. Do you know how hard it is to sign things with hooves?

sit_tight_gerald.speaker: gerald
sit_tight_gerald.text: More waiting. Right. Fine. I'll just... sit here. Being homeless. Without a labyrinth.

sit_tight_gerald_2.speaker: gerald
sit_tight_gerald_2.text: Don't mind me. I'll be FINE.

post_case_greeting.speaker: gerald
post_case_greeting.text: Oh Mr. Grimshaw! You're really going to find my labyrinth! I feel so much better!

post_case_2.speaker: gerald
post_case_2.text: Well, not GOOD exactly. More like 'slightly less catastrophically anxious.' But for a minotaur, that's practically euphoria!

choices.text: Tell me everything about the day it disappeared.

disappearance_details.speaker: gerald
disappearance_details.text: Right, so, it was Tuesday. I know because Tuesday is thread day at the market. Minotaur thing, don't ask.

disappearance_2.speaker: gerald
disappearance_2.text: I was gone maybe two hours. When I came back — empty field. Not even a wall. Just that business card and a faint smell of cheap cologne.

disappearance_3.text: Bastardo's signature scent. 'Eau de Charlatan.' I'd recognise it anywhere.

disappearance_4.speaker: gerald
disappearance_4.text: So you WILL find him? And my labyrinth?

disappearance_5.text: I'll find him. Whether the labyrinth is still in one piece... well, we'll cross that bridge when we come to it.

keep_posted_bye.speaker: gerald
keep_posted_bye.text: Thank you, Mr. Grimshaw. You're a good man. A bit scruffy and you smell of kebab, but a good man.
